_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-27 Transport of Adult Ventilator-Dependent Patients (Revised 6/27/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-27: Transport of Adult Ventilator-Dependent Patients — MDHHS Approved 6/27/2023
purpose: Govern transport of adult patients dependent on mechanical ventilation. BLS units may transport on the patient's own ventilator under specific conditions. ALS units may use a transport ventilator. Waveform capnography is mandatory for transport ventilators. PEEP > 5 cmH2O requires MICU or air medical transport or hospital staff accompaniment.

sections.title: BLS Transport of Ventilator-Dependent Patients
points: BLS units may transport a ventilator-dependent patient on the patient's OWN (home or facility) ventilator if ALL of the following conditions are met:
1. A trained caregiver who is familiar with the patient's ventilator accompanies the patient throughout transport
2. Capnography (waveform) OR numeric capnometry (if waveform not available) is in use
3. The transport is a SCHEDULED transport or a low-acuity 9-1-1 BLS call
BVM must be immediately accessible throughout transport
BLS should NOT manage ventilator adjustments independently — defer to the trained caregiver

sections.title: ALS Transport Ventilator Use
points: ALS units: all paramedics on the unit must be trained on the transport ventilator being used
VERIFY endotracheal tube or SGA placement with WAVEFORM CAPNOGRAPHY before placing patient on transport ventilator
Recheck lung sounds AND waveform capnography after every patient movement (e.g., loading into ambulance, transferring to hospital cot)
ACCEPTABLE ventilator modes: Assist Control (AC) and Synchronized Intermittent Mandatory Ventilation (SIMV)
Keep BVM immediately accessible at all times

sections.title: PEEP Considerations
points: PEEP > 5 cmH2O: patient requires MICU (Mobile Intensive Care Unit) or air medical transport OR a hospital staff member must accompany the patient
If none of these resources are available: contact Medical Control for guidance

sections.title: Additional Considerations
points: Newly intubated patients: obtain venous blood gas (VBG) when available to guide ventilator settings before transport
Waveform capnography is mandatory for ALL patients on a transport ventilator
Target ETCO2: 35–45 mmHg (unless protocol or medical condition indicates otherwise)

deterioration_flags: ETCO2 drops to 0 on transport ventilator — follow ETCO2 zero protocol: check tubing/connections; verify placement; remove advanced airway if zero persists
PEEP > 5 cmH2O — MICU or air medical or hospital staff accompaniment required
Patient deteriorating on transport ventilator — switch to manual BVM; reassess airway placement; contact Medical Control
scope_notes: BLS may transport on patient's OWN ventilator IF: trained caregiver accompanies + capnography in use + scheduled or low-acuity BLS call
ALS: all paramedics must be trained on transport ventilator used
Verify ETT/SGA placement with waveform capnography before placing on transport vent
Recheck lung sounds and capnography after every patient movement
PEEP > 5 cmH2O: MICU or air medical or hospital staff required
Waveform capnography mandatory for all transport ventilator use
BVM immediately accessible at all times
